creator: emaste@freebsd.org
text:
shurd pointed me to https://sourceforge.net/p/wsjt/mailman/wsjt-devel/thread/972b4b76-d37a-16d0-d362-90fde0244104%40classdesign.com/#msg36196649, and when I look at decoder.f90.o it should be requesting exec stack:

$ readelf -S work/.build/wsjtx-prefix/src/wsjtx-build/CMakeFiles/wsjt_fort.dir/lib/decoder.f90.o
There are 20 section headers, starting at offset 0x10938:

Section Headers:
  [Nr] Name              Type            Address          Off    Size   ES Flg Lk Inf Al
...
  [14] .note.GNU-stack   PROGBITS        0000000000000000 0077d8 000000 00   X  0   0  1
...
Key to Flags:
  W (write), A (alloc), X (execute), M (merge), S (strings), I (info),
  L (link order), O (extra OS processing required), G (group), T (TLS),
  C (compressed), x (unknown), o (OS specific), E (exclude),
  R (retain), l (large), p (processor specific)

So it looks like this has somehow been lost when linking the binary.
